Appearances: Ernesto Floresca (650.238.7824) plaintiff and moving party appearing in propria persona; No other appearances.

Plaintiff is to give notice of the new hearing date. Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2016 Set For Trial On Oct-03-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-16-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JAMES MCELROY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Mr. Floresca has adequately alleged that, but for Mr. McElroy's asserted malpractice, Mr. Floresca would have been able to enter into an agreement with the US Attorney's office providing for a more favorable sentence than he received. The court takes judicial notice of the sentencing guidelines and the existence of the plea agreement and sentencing memorandum, but not the truth of the contents of the plea agreement or sentencing memorandum. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-09-2015 Continued To Mar-30-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-26-2015 Continued To Nov-25-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT JAMES MCELROY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. A claim for professional negligence must plead facts showing not only a duty and breach, but also that but for the alleged malpractice, it is more likely than not that the plaintiff would have obtained a more favorable result. (Shopoff & Cavallo LLP v. Hyon (2008) 167 Cal.App.4th 1489, 1509-11.) Plaintiff asserts that if McElroy had read the sentencing memorandum properly, Plaintiff would have been able to challenge his sentence and restitution fine. He does not plead, however, facts showing that he actually would have obtained a better result in the absence of McElroy's negligence. Sentencing is within the discretion of the trial judge; it is not clear on what basis Plaintiff believes he could have obtained a more lenient sentence absent McElroy's admitted negligence. Plaintiff may amend his complaint to plead facts, if he is able to do so, showing how he would have avoided going to jail or paying the restitution fine but for McElroy's negligence. Defendant's argument that the claim is barred by the statute of limitations is rejected. Plaintiff's claim stems from Defendant's misreading of the government's sentencing memorandum in February 2014. Because his suit was filed in November 2014, it was within the 1 year statute of limitations period. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-21-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF ERNESTO FLORESCA'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is DENIED. Proof of service establishes the demurrer was sent to Plaintiff. Plaintiff obviously received notice at some point, or he would not have known to oppose the demurrer, or to file this motion to strike. Plaintiff does not plead how or when he did get notice, any prejudice, or otherwise show why the demurrer should be stricken. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
